commands.author: snowzlmbot

target.title: fix(telegram): render progress draft rows as plain readable text
target.body:
## Summary Fixes #95002. Telegram progress draft previews now keep their canonical preview text plain and readable instead of making ordinary tool/progress rows depend on Telegram HTML/code wrappers. Before this change, progress drafts asserted payloads like: ```html Shelling
🛠️ Exec command false exit 2 ``` After this change, the same draft is represented as readable plain text: ```text Shelling 🛠️ exit 2; command false ``` ## Problem The Telegram progress draft renderer used ``, ``, ``, and `
` for transient progress rows. This made command/tool progress noisy even when HTML parsed successfully, and any fallback/sanitized rendering could expose transport markup to the user instead of a useful status line. ## Changes - Stop wrapping Telegram progress draft lines in Markdown backticks/code spans. - Normalize Telegram progress draft rows to plain readable text first. - Keep non-rich Telegram progress drafts as plain text payloads instead of `parseMode: "HTML"` payloads. - Preserve rich-message support by continuing to build escaped HTML from the same normalized plain line model when rich messages are enabled. - Update Telegram dispatch tests to assert readable progress draft text instead of HTML/code payloads. ## Verification Local targeted checks: ```bash node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-telegram.config.ts telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-telegram.config.ts telegram/src/draft-stream.test.ts telegram/src/lane-delivery.test.ts git diff --check ``` Results: - `telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts`: 129 passed - `telegram/src/draft-stream.test.ts`: 46 passed - `telegram/src/lane-delivery.test.ts`: 41 passed - `git diff --check`: passed ## Risk Low-to-moderate Telegram presentation risk: - This intentionally changes Telegram progress draft preview text from HTML-formatted rows to plain readable rows. - Final answer rendering is not changed. - Normal Telegram markdown/HTML answer rendering remains outside this progress-draft path. - Rich progress previews still get escaped HTML generated from the normalized plain line model. ## Not addressed This PR does not change Telegram draft materialization policy, including first-preview debounce/minimum length or progress-mode answer-lane suppression. That separate behavior is tracked in #95004 because it involves a product tradeoff between notification noise and Web preview parity.
